title: Story narration improvements: remove speaker labels, add voice styles, and update Miles alt text

requirements.summary: Remove explicit speaker-label prefixes from generated narration while keeping dialogue/thought/caption/SFX/scene narration coherent.
acceptanceCriteria: When narrating any panel part of type "dialogue", the spoken line does not contain the substring " says:" and does not prepend the speaker name by default.
When narrating any panel part of type "thought", the spoken line does not contain the substring " thinks:" and does not prepend the speaker name by default.
Captions, scene text, and SFX continue to be narrated in a reasonable, understandable way (no runtime errors; narration still produces a non-empty script for the existing story).

file_operations.description: Update narration script derivation so dialogue/thought lines are speakable without speaker-label prefixes (remove patterns like "<speaker> says:" and "<speaker> thinks:") while keeping captions/scene/SFX speakable; adjust the script item shape if needed to carry speaker metadata separately for later voice selection.

Wire updated deriveNarrationScript output into narration playback so the spoken text for dialogue/thought is natural and does not prepend speaker names by default; ensure no runtime errors and script remains non-empty for existing STORY_PANELS. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add per-character voice selection (Miles, Jefferson, and default) plus basic rate/pitch controls (if supported) and apply them to Web Speech narration without reload.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI includes a section in the Story Narration area for selecting a voice for Miles, a voice for Jefferson, and a default voice for all other speakers.
If the browser provides multiple speechSynthesis voices, the dropdowns list them and selecting a voice changes the actual SpeechSynthesisUtterance.voice used during narration.
If the browser provides no selectable voices (or voices are not yet loaded), the UI shows a clear English message and narration still works with the browser default voice.
Changing voice settings does not require page reload and takes effect for the next started narration session.
The implementation does not claim to perfectly imitate copyrighted character voices; it only uses available system/browser TTS voices.

file_operations.description: Create a React hook that loads and tracks available browser SpeechSynthesis voices (including handling delayed loading via the voiceschanged event) and exposes a stable list + a "voices ready" signal for the UI to render dropdown options or a clear fallback message.

Extend narration playback to accept voice-style configuration (per-speaker voice selection plus optional rate/pitch) and apply it when constructing each SpeechSynthesisUtterance (set utterance.voice when available, and set utterance.rate/utterance.pitch within safe ranges); ensure changes take effect on the next start without reload.

Add a "Voice Style" section inside the existing Story Narration panel: dropdowns for Miles/Jefferson/Default voices (using the new useSpeechSynthesisVoices hook) and optional rate/pitch controls; show a clear English message when voices are unavailable/not yet loaded while keeping narration functional with default voice. Use existing shadcn-ui components already in the file (e.g., Button/Alert/Badge) for consistent styling where applicable; Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure deriveNarrationScript provides enough metadata (e.g., speaker identity when present) so the narration hook can pick Miles/Jefferson/default voice mappings while still keeping the generated spoken line free of speaker-label prefixes.

requirements.summary: Update Miles visual description in story panel alt text (opening panel) to explicitly mention he is Black with curly hair while keeping dialogue intact.
acceptanceCriteria: In the first story panel illustrationAlt (spiderverse-panel-01...), the description explicitly mentions Miles is Black and has curly hair.
No story dialogue content is removed; the line "Why am I stuck to the ceiling?!" remains present in the data and continues to be narrated.

file_operations.description: Update the first story panel (spiderverse-panel-01.dim_1024x768.png) illustrationAlt text to explicitly describe Miles as Black with curly hair; do not remove or alter story dialogue content (ensure the existing "Why am I stuck to the ceiling?!" line remains unchanged elsewhere in the data).
